breakdown.note: Curriculum is public, but materials and assessment require institutional login.
CMSP platform is robust and supports millions of concurrent users.
Complete lesson scripts — teacher receives exact content and projection materials.

São Paulo.shortSummary: Mandatory 'Tecnologia e Inovação' subject delivered via private platforms with fully scripted lessons. Materials and assessments are locked behind institutional login.
São Paulo.summary: A centralized platform-based model utilizing pre-defined scripts and pre-made slides through partnerships with private platforms. While focused on large-scale delivery, SEDUC restricts most pedagogical content to logged-in users, creating barriers to transparency and external collaboration.

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: Currículo Paulista — Educação Digital e Midiática (Aligned with CIEB standards)

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: 100% Implemented across the state public network.

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: Implemented as a mandatory curriculum in 2020 through the 'Inova Educação' Program.

breakdown.note: Curriculum and materials fully open; assessment results are only partially available.
Hosted on permanent Google Drive links — resilient and always accessible.
Detailed lesson guides and videos, though not all classes have editable slides.

Minas Gerais.shortSummary: Mandatory 'Digital Education' subject since 2026 with fully open, state-authored materials. Covers AI and Data Science, though assessment results are not yet public.
Minas Gerais.summary: Recently implemented computer science curriculum focused on digital literacy and Artificial Intelligence. The model prioritizes in-house audiovisual content through the 'Digital Training School' (Escola de Formação Digital), which is open to the public. However, practical implementation remains dependent on local infrastructure and assessment results are not yet publicly available.

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: Referencial Curricular Computação na Educação Básica

breakdown.note: Classroom materials are restricted; no public assessment guides available.
'Escola Tá ON' portal suffers frequent crashes and 404 errors.
Most technically deep framework of the four — covers AI and Data Science in detail.

Espírito Santo.shortSummary: Computational Thinking embedded transversally in Mathematics — no standalone subject. The curriculum is technically deep but the main resource platform suffers chronic instability.
Espírito Santo.summary: The state does not offer a standalone computing subject, integrating Computational Thinking as a transversal competency within the Mathematics curriculum. While the curricular framework is updated and broadly available for public consultation, access to practical teaching materials and lesson plans is hindered by the constant instability of the 'Escola Tá ON' platform.

details.label: Curriculum Framework
details.text: Integrated into Mathematics and its Technologies Curriculum

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: Not mandatory | Integration with Math Curriculum started in 2024

breakdown.note: High-quality curriculum and matrices are public, but pedagogical guides (Stations) are fragmented or require login.
Official PDFs are stable, but the Anísio Teixeira and 'Escola Tá ON' platforms face recurring downtime.
Deep pedagogical structure in the Knowledge Stations model, with transdisciplinary milestones and AI integration.

Bahia.shortSummary: Transdisciplinary 'Knowledge Stations' model with a Computational Thinking component focused on local problem-solving and AI. Access is enabled via the Conectar project and the Google ecosystem.
Bahia.summary: Bahia adopts the 'Knowledge Stations' model in High School, featuring a 'Computational Thinking' component focused on experimental praxis and territorial problem-solving. The state strategy emphasizes digital sovereignty and the use of AI, integrating technologies transdisciplinarily and utilizing the Conectar project to enable access via the Google ecosystem and mobile devices.

details.label: Subject
details.text: Knowledge Stations IV – Computational Thinking (HS) | Body Culture and Technologies (MS)

details.label: Weekly Hours
details.text: 3 classes/week (120 annual hours) in HS | 0h standalone in MS

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: 100% Implemented in curriculum / Expansion phase in Full-Time schools

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: Mandatory since 2022 (Consolidation of the New High School)

breakdown.note: Itinerary materials are public, but no assessment data was found.
Government sites are generally stable, with occasional outages.
Only high-level competency lists — no practical classroom scripts provided.

Rio de Janeiro.shortSummary: Technology itineraries exist only in full-time schools — no universal computing subject for the general stream. Teaching materials are locked behind authenticated portals.
Rio de Janeiro.summary: The state does not offer a universal computing component in its general education stream, providing technology itineraries only within full-time integral schools. Furthermore, pedagogical guides and teaching materials are locked behind authenticated portals, preventing public access and external oversight.

details.label: Implementation Status
details.text: ~25% Implemented (restricted to full-time and technical schools)

details.label: Policy Start Date
details.text: Not mandatory for general stream | New High School implementation began in 2022

Rio de Janeiro.footnote: * No specific subject exists in the general network; computing is treated as transversal content. In the New High School's technology itinerary, it includes the subjects 'Technology, Innovation, and Environment' and 'STEAM Culture'.
